qText: A drum contains a blend of three chemicals: P, Q, and R, in the ratio of 3 : 5 : 2, 60 litres of this blend are removed, and then 12 litres of chemical P and 8 litres of chemical Q are poured into the drum. If the resultant quantity of chemical Q is 40 litres more than the resultant quantity of chemical P, what was the initial total quantity of the mixture in the drum (in litres) ?

A milkman mixes water in pure milk and offers the mixture for sale at the original cost price of milk. If he mixes 0.25 liters of water in every 6.50 liters of milk and sells the entire mixture at the price of pure milk, what is his profit percentage?
A 45-litre mixture of petrol and kerosene is in the ratio 5 : 4. Some of it is removed and replaced with pure petrol. The final ratio becomes 7 : 4. How much of the mixture was replaced ?

Two different quantities of the same solution having ingredients A and B are stored in two different containers. In the first container, there are 252 litre of A and 441 litre of B. In the second container, the total quantity of the solution was 1188 litre. How much of the solution in the second container was made up of ingredient B ?
A bucket contains a mixture of milk and water in the proportion of 7 : 5. If 9 litres of milk is replaced by water, then the ratio becomes 7 : 9. How much milk was there in the bucket initially ?

A drink is made by mixing water and juice in the ratio 9 : 7. If x litres of water and 2x litres of juice are mixed in 160 litres of drink, then the new ratio becomes 13 : 15. The quantity of drink (in litres) is:

80 litres of a mixture of spirit and water in the ratio 7 : 9 is present in a container A. 20 litres of the mixture is transferred to another container B. Then the container A is filled with 20 litres of water. Then 32 litres of the mixture is again transferred to container B. The ratio of water to spirit of the mixture in container B is equal to:

At a certain time in a park, the number of heads and the number of legs of monkeys and human visitors were counted, and it was found that there were 54 heads and 148 legs. Find the number of monkeys in the park.

A milk vendor has 2 vessels of milk and water. The first contains 80% milk and the rest is water. The second contains 50% milk. How much mixture (in L) should he mix from each of the vessels so as to get 9 litres of mixture such that the ratio of water to milk is 1 : 2 ? (Write your answer in the order of vessel 1 and vessel 2)

A blended tea in a barrel contains Assam, Darjeeling and Nilgiri Hills in the ratio 2 : 3 : 1. Another barrel contains Darjeeling, Nilgiri Hills and Kangra Valley in the ratio 5 : 4 : 3. If equal weights of both blended tea mix together to make a new brand, then the weight of Kangra Valley per kilogram in the new brand is:

From a cask of milk containing 64 liters, 8 liters are drawn out and the cask is filled up with water. If the same process is repeated a second, then a third time, what will be the proportion of milk to water in the resulting mixture ?

A jar contains 35% syrup in the mixture of syrup and water. A part of this mixture is replaced by another mixture containing 20% syrup. Now, in the new mixture, the percentage of syrup is found to be 24%. What is the ratio in which these mixtures were mixed to obtain the new mixture ?

In a solution of water and milk, the quantity of milk is double the quantity of water. To pass the quality standards, the solution must contain 90% milk in it. If the volume of the solution is v, then how much pure milk in litres is to be added to the solution to pass the quality standard.

Alloy X contains 70% copper and 30% zinc. Alloy Y contains 40% copper, 25% zinc and 35% aluminum. Alloy X and Y are mixed in the ratio of 1 : 3. What is the ratio of copper and zinc in the newly formed alloy ?

A jar contains a mixture of milk and water, having 50% water. A part of this mixture is replaced by another mixture having 29% water. The percentage of water in the jar after replacement is 36%. What fraction of the original mixture was replaced ?

Two vessels of equal capacity, and both completely filled, contain mixtures of milk and water in the ratio 5 : 1 and 6 : 5, respectively. Both the mixtures are now mixed thoroughly. What is the ratio of milk to water in the new mixture so obtained ?

Babulal runs a juice corner outside a park and sells giloy juice (giloy + water) in the morning. Initially he had 19 litres of juice, which had giloy and water in the 1 ratio 2 : 9. He sold 2 litres of juice. 2 Later, in order to dilute it, he added some water and the ratio of giloy and water became 1 : 5. How much water was added ?

Alloy A contains metal x and y only in the ratio 5 : 2 and alloy B contains these metals in the ratio 3 : 4. Alloy C is prepared by mixing A and B in the ratio 4 : 5. The percentage of x in alloy C is:

Two bottles of same capacity are 1 35% and 33 % full of orange juice, 3 respectively. They are filled up completely with apple juice and then the contents of both bottles are emptied into another vessel. The percentage of apple juice in the mixture is:

A vessel contains a 32 litre solution of acid and water in which the ratio of acid and water is 5 : 3. If 12 litres of the solution are taken out and 7 1 2 litres of water are added to it, then what is the ratio of acid and water in the resulting solution ?
The ratios of copper to Zinc in alloys A and B are 3 : 4 and 5 : 9 respectively. A and B are taken in the ratio 2 : 3 and melted to form a new alloy C. What is the ratio of copper to Zinc in C ?

A milkman uses three containers for selling milk, their capacities being 40L, 30L and 20L respectively. He fills respectively 87.5%, 80% and 90% of the containers with a mix of milk and water in the ratios, 3 : 2, 5 : 1 and 7 : 2 respectively. What is the ratio of total quantity of milk to that of water carried by him ?
Fresh fruit contains 68% water and dry fruit contains 20% water. How much dry fruit can be obtained from 100 kg of fresh fruit
A 80-liter mixture contains Milk and water in the ratio 5 : 3. How much water (in liters) must be added to this mixture to change the ratio of Milk to water to 2 : 3 ?
Two Lentils priced at ‚Çπ90 per kg and ‚Çπ220 per kg are blended and sold at ‚Çπ250 per kg, achieving a profit margin of 25%. What is the ratio of the two lentils in the mixture ?

A 200-liter solution contains milk and water in the ratio 3 : 2. Some of this solution is removed and replaced with pure milk. If the final ratio of milk and water becomes 7 : 3, how many liters of solution were replaced ?
Approximately how many kg of pulses costing ‚Çπ45 per kg should be mixed with 24 kg of pulses costing ‚Çπ32 per kg so as to make a profit of 15% by selling the mixture at ‚Çπ43.70 per kg ?
From a 64-liter container of pure milk, 8 liters are removed and replaced by water. This operation is performed a total of three times. What is the final quantity of milk remaining in the container ?

A juice vendor mixes water with pure mango juice. If he sells the mixture at the cost price of pure mango juice and makes a profit of 20%, what is the ratio of water to pure mango juice in the mixture, respectively ?

A 70 litre mixture contains milk and water in the ratio 4 : 3. If 7 litres of this mixture is removed and 10 litres of pure milk is added in its place, what will be the new ratio of milk and water in the mixture?
A 30-litres solution of alcohol and water has 20% water. How many litres of alcohol must be added to the solution to make the water content 15% ?

Two containers of the same volume are 30% and 50% full of milk, respectively. They are then filled completely with water. If the contents of both containers are mixed in a larger vessel, what is the percentage of water in the final mixture ?
A solution consists of milk and water in the ratio 3 : 5. If 8 litres of water is added, the new ratio becomes 3 : 7. What is the original quantity of milk in the solution ?

A tank has a mixture of solutions A, B, and C in the respective ratio of 5 : 3 : 2. 10 litres of this mixture is drained out, and subsequently, 5 litres of solution A and 3 litres of solution C are added to the tank. If the resultant quantity of solution A is 25 litres more than the resultant quantity of solution B, what was the initial quantity of mixture in the tank (in litres) ?

A container holds a mixture of three liquids P, Q, and R in ratio 4 : 6 : 10. If 6 liters of liquid P, 12 liters of liquid Q, and a certain amount of liquid R are added to the container, the new ratio of liquids P, Q, and R becomes 6 : 10 : 16. Find the quantity (in liters) of liquid R added.

A cistern contains 200 liters of pure syrup. 10 liters of syrup are drawn out and replaced by water. Then, 10 liters of the mixture are drawn out and replaced by water again. What is the ratio of water to syrup in the final mixture ?

A container holds a mixture of Mango, Apple, and Pomegranate juices in the ratio of 5 : 7 : 4 respectively. 24 litres of this mixture is taken out, and then 10 litres of Mango juice and 6 litres of Pomegranate juice are added to the vessel. If the resultant quantity of apple juice is 14 litres more than the resultant quantity of Mango juice, what was the initial quantity of mixture in the vessel (in litres) ?
3 A tank holds 7 liters of water. If 4 3.375 liters are drained twice, how much water remains ?

Two containers, A and B, contain mixtures of alcohol and water. Container A has alcohol and water in the ratio 2 : 1, while Container B has them in the ratio 4 : 3. If 9 liters are drawn from Container A and 14 liters from Container B, and the contents are mixed in a third container, what is the ratio of alcohol to water in the new mixture ?

Two distinct varieties of wheat, valued at ‚Çπ80 per kilogram and ‚Çπ120 per kilogram respectively, are combined in an unknown ratio. The resulting mixture is sold at ‚Çπ130 per kilogram, yielding a profit of 25% on the aggregate cost price. Determine the ratio in which the two wheat varieties were blended.

A 120 - litre mixture contains milk and water in the ratio 5 : 3. If 'x' litres of milk and 'x' litres of water are added to the mixture, the new ratio of milk to water becomes 3 : 2. What is the value of 'x' ?

A barrel contains a mixture of alcohol and water in the ratio 5 : 3 respectively. If 24 litres of water are added to it, the ratio of alcohol to water becomes 5 : 9. Find the initial total quantity of the mixture in the barrel.

Two containers of equal capacity filled with juice and water. The first contains juice and water in the ratio 2 : 3, and the second in the ratio 3 : 2. The mixtures are then combined. What is the ratio of juice to water in the resulting mixture ?
A Barthan has 90 litres of a solution with sugar and soda in the ratio 7 : 2. If 18 litres of the solution are replaced with soda, what is the new ratio?
In a 60-liter mixture of apple juice and water, the amount of water is 20 liters. How many liters of water should be added to make the ratio of juice to water 2 : 3 ?

A vessel contains milk and water in the ratio 6 : 5. If 10 liters of milk are removed and 9 liters of water are added, the new ratio becomes 2 : 3. What was the original quantity of the mixture in the vessel ?
A mixture of 200 litres contains acid and water in the ratio 3:5. If 40 litres of the mixture is replaced with water, what will be the percentage of acid in the final mixture ?
A 32-litre mixture of milk and water contains milk and water in the ratio 3 : 5. How much water must be added to make the ratio 3 : 6 ?

An alloy contains gold and silver in the ratio 5 : 3. A second alloy contains gold and silver in the ratio 3 : 1. What quantity of the first alloy must be added to 40 kg of the second alloy to form a new alloy containing gold and silver in the ratio 4 : 2 ?
A merchant wants to mix two types of tea priced at Rs. 400/kg and Rs. 340/kg. In what ratio should they be mixed to sell the mixture at Rs. 370/kg without profit or loss ?

A solution of 150 litres has a 40% salt concentration. How many litres of water must be added to reduce the concentration to 20%, and then how much pure salt must be added to bring the concentration back to 50% ?
optText: ‚Äã(a) Add 150 L of water, then 180 L of salt‚Äã
‚Äã(b) Add 90 L of water, then 170 L of salt‚Äã
‚Äã(c) Add 170 L of water, then 30 L of salt‚Äã
‚Äã(d) Add 150 L of water, then 10 L of salt‚Äã

qText: Two numbers are in the ratio 8 : 9 If 8 is added to each, the new ratio becomes 17 : 19. What are the original numbers ?

A container has a 80-litre mixture of milk and water in the ratio 3 : 1. 12 litres is removed and replaced with water. Then 12 litres is removed again and replaced with milk. What is the final percentage of water ?
A 40-litre mixture of milk and water contains milk and water in the ratio 3 : 1. Some quantity of water is added, changing the ratio to 3 : 5. What is the quantity of water added ?
A 100-litre container of pure milk undergoes a 10-litre removal and 10-litre water replacement process, repeated 3 times. Approximately how much original milk remains?

A container has 100 litres of a mixture of acid and water in the ratio 3 : 2. 10% of the mixture is taken out and replaced with water. This operation is repeated once more. What is the final percentage of acid in the mixture ?

A bag contains red, blue, and green marbles in the ratio 4 : 5 : 6 . if 6 red marbles are removed and 3 green marbles are added, the new ratio becomes 2 : 5 : 7. How many red marbles were originally in the bag ?

A tank contains 150 litres of liquid, 30% of which is alcohol. If 20 litres of this mixture is replaced by another liquid containing 40 % alcohol, what is the percentage of alcohol in the resulting solution ?

A 80-litres mixture of acid and water contains acid and water in the ratio 4 : 1. 10 litres of this mixture is removed and replaced with another solution of acid and water in the ratio 1 : 4. What is the final ratio of acid to water in the resulting mixture ?
A 136 - litre container of pure milk undergoes a 17-litre removal and 17-litre water replacement process, repeated 2 times. Approximately how much original milk remains?

From a 54-liter container of pure spirit, 9 liters are removed and replaced by water. This operation is performed a total of three times. What is the final quantity of spirit remaining in the container ?

A cistern contains 100 liters of pure milk . 20 liters of milk are drawn out and replaced by water. Then, 20 liters of the mixture are drawn out and replaced by water again. What is the ratio of water to milk in the final mixture ?

Malika bought two varieties of pulses, costing Rs. 75 per kg and Rs. 125 per kg each, and mixed them in some ratio. Then she sold the mixture at Rs. 121 per kg. Making a profit of 10 percent. What was the ratio of the mixture?

Two varieties of rice are mixed. The first variety costs ‚Çπ60 per kg and the second variety costs ‚Çπ90 per kg. In what ratio should these be mixed so that by selling the mixture at ‚Çπ84 per kg, a profit of 20% is gained?
A merchant mixes 80 kg of coffee at ‚Çπ100/kg with a dearer variety at ‚Çπ140/kg. He sells the blend at ‚Çπ138/kg and gains 15% profit. How many kg of the dearer variety did he add?

A cistern contains 120 liters of pure milk . 30 liters of milk are drawn out and replaced by water. Then, 30 liters of the mixture are drawn out and replaced by water again. What is the ratio of water to milk in the final mixture ?
A solution of 150 liters contains 30% acid. How much pure acid (in liters) must be added to make the solution 50% acid?
A shopkeeper mixes 45 kg of rice at ‚Çπ65/kg with another grade at ‚Çπ95/kg. He sells the mixture at ‚Çπ80/kg, making a 1 profit of 14 %. How many kg of the 7 dearer rice did he use?

A mixture contains juice and water in the ratio 4 : 3. If 21 litres of water are added to this mixture, the ratio of juice to water becomes 5 : 4. What was the initial quantity of the mixture (in litres)?
A 60-liter mixture contains acid and water in the ratio 7 : 3. How much water (in liters) must be added to this mixture to change the ratio of acid to water to 4 : 5?
If two kinds of resins costing ‚Çπ160 per kg and ‚Çπ210 per kg are mixed in the ratio of 2 : 3, then find the cost (in ‚Çπ) of the mixture per kg.

A chef has two cans of acids and water mixture. He combines the contents of both cans in a beaker. The resulting mixture has half water and half acid. What is the quantity in which he mixed the contents of can 1 and can 2, given that can 2 has an acid-to-water ratio of 2 : 3 and can 1 has an acid-to-water ratio of 5 : 3 ?

A vessel contains 80 litres of a mixture of alcohol and water in the ratio of 3 : 7. If 40 litres of the mixture is taken out from the vessel and replaced with 40 litres of water, then the ratio of alcohol and water becomes:

A can contains a mixture of two liquids, grape juice and pineapple juice in the ratio 13 : 11. When 8 litres of the mixture is drawn off, and the can is filled with pineapple juice, the ratio becomes 13 : 14. How many litres of pineapple juice was initially contained in the can ?

A shopkeeper of a subsidised canteen mixed three varieties of rice, X, Y and Z, in the ratio of 4 : 1 : 3 to make a packet of 4 kg of rice. The cost prices per kg of X, Y and Z varieties of rice are ‚Çπ2, ‚Çπ4 and ‚Çπ6, respectively. What is the cost price (in ‚Çπ) of the packet of rice ?
Rice worth ‚Çπ84 per kg and ‚Çπ96 per kg is mixed with a third variety of rice in the ratio 1 : 1 : 2. If the mixture is worth ‚Çπ99 per kg, the price of the third variety per kg is:

The concentrations of three acids, A, B, and C, are specified as 20%, 30%, and 40%, respectively. They are blended in a ratio of 3 : 5 : a, yielding a solution with a concentration of 30%. What is the value of 'a' ?
A merchant mixes two varieties of sugar of 5 kg and 3 kg, which costs him ‚Çπ4 and ‚Çπ6 per kg, respectively. What is the cost price (in ‚Çπ) of the mixture per kg
A shopkeeper mixes two types of rice costing ‚Çπ250/5 kg and ‚Çπ280/5 kg in the ratio 7 : 8. Find the net cost of the resultant mixture (per kg).

2 varieties of tea powders worth ‚Çπ250/kg and ‚Çπ300/kg are mixed with a third variety of tea powder in the ratio 1 : 1 : 2. If the mixture is worth ‚Çπ350/kg, find the price of the third variety tea powder.
15 kg of ‚Çπ10 per kg wheat is mixed with 5 kg of another type of wheat to get a mixture costing ‚Çπ30 per kg. Find the price (per kg) of the costlier wheat.

A mango juice is made by mixing water and mango concentrate in the ratio 9 : 7. If x litres of water and 3x litres of mango concentrate is mixed in 160 litres of mango juice, then the new ratio becomes 13 : 14. What is the quantity of the new mango juice (in litres)?
In what ratio, sugar costing ‚Çπ78 per kg be mixed with sugar costing ‚Çπ46 per kg such that by selling the mixture at ‚Çπ75 per kg there is a gain of 20% ?

A man buys 10 kg of wheat at a rate of ‚Çπ26/kg. The wheat is mixed with 6 kg of other good quality of wheat to get a mixture at a price of ‚Çπ35/kg. The price of good quality wheat per kg (in ‚Çπ) is:
Half litre of a solution contains 15% of alcohol. To change the alcohol concentration to 50%, find the quantity of alcohol to be mixed.
How much water should be added 1 to 60 liters of milk at 1 liters a rupee 2 7 so as to have a mixture worth 1 liters a 8 rupee ?
Ramu mixes 2 litres of water at 100¬∞ C and 18 litres of water at 32¬∞ C. What temperature will the water have after mixing ?
